label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about why the employer made all those criticisms about Julia Vassilyevna's work. The options are A to D. So the story is about a man calculating his governess's pay. He starts off saying they agreed on thirty rubles a month, but she says forty. He insists on thirty. Then he starts deducting various things like Sundays, holidays, days the kids were sick, her having a toothache, breaking a teacup, the kid climbing a tree and tearing his jacket, the maid stealing shoes, and some money he claims he gave her. Each time he subtracts more, reducing her pay. Julia doesn't protest any of it, even when he's being unfair. At the end, he gives her eleven rubles and she says "merci". He gets angry and says he's cheated her and gives her the full eighty rubles instead. She still just says "merci" and leaves. The man reflects that it's easy to crush the weak. Looking at the options: A) She neglected her duties. But in the story, the employer is making up or exaggerating the deductions. For example, the teacup, the kid climbing the tree, maybe even the days off. Also, Julia didn't actually neglect her duties
